title: Geometry, Scale, Similarity, and Spatial Planning · Worked Case: Scale Drawing
summary: Create a scale plan for an accessible room layout with paths, clearances, paint, and flooring. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg scale drawing increment in an accessible scale floor plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use a consistent scale factor to move between drawings and actual dimensions.
Choose volume or surface-area models for capacity, covering, and packaging tasks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Scale Drawing evidence
steps.instruction: A rectangular working area measures 8 m by 6 m. Calculate its area. Use the result to decide Scale Drawing for an accessible scale floor plan.
steps.why: The Scale Drawing result for an accessible scale floor plan is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Multiply perpendicular lengths and square the length unit: 8 m × 6 m. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use a consistent scale factor to move between drawings and actual dimensions.
